questions.question: Explain why a quadratic equation can have at most two roots.
questions.answer: Because the degree of a quadratic equation is two.

questions.question: Explain why the discriminant is important in solving quadratic equations.
questions.answer: Because it determines the nature of the roots without solving the equation.

questions.question: Why does a parabola touch the x-axis when roots are equal?
questions.answer: Because the discriminant is zero.

questions.question: Explain why a quadratic equation with D < 0 has no real roots.
questions.answer: Because the square root of a negative number is not real.

questions.question: Why does the parabola open upwards when a > 0?
questions.answer: Because the leading coefficient is positive.

questions.question: Why does the parabola open downwards when a < 0?
questions.answer: Because the leading coefficient is negative.

questions.question: Explain why the vertex gives maximum or minimum value.
questions.answer: Because it is the turning point of the parabola.

questions.question: Why is quadratic formula called a universal method?
questions.answer: Because it works for all quadratic equations.

questions.question: Explain why graphical method gives only approximate roots.
questions.answer: Because accuracy depends on scale and drawing.

questions.question: Why is completing the square helpful in finding vertex?
questions.answer: Because it converts the equation into vertex form.

questions.question: Why does x² + 1 = 0 have no real solution?
questions.answer: Because x² is always non-negative.

questions.question: Explain why roots are equidistant from the axis of symmetry.
questions.answer: Because the parabola is symmetric.

questions.question: Why are quadratic equations useful in optimisation problems?
questions.answer: Because they help find maximum or minimum values.

questions.question: Explain why the graph of a quadratic equation is continuous.
questions.answer: Because it has no breaks or gaps.

questions.question: Why does the discriminant become zero for perfect square quadratics?
questions.answer: Because they have equal roots.

questions.question: Why are quadratic equations used in physics?
questions.answer: Because many motions follow parabolic paths.

questions.question: Explain why quadratic equations cannot model exponential growth.
questions.answer: Because exponential growth does not have constant second differences.

questions.question: Why does changing b shift the parabola horizontally?
questions.answer: Because b affects the axis of symmetry.

questions.question: Why does changing c shift the parabola vertically?
questions.answer: Because c is the y-intercept.

questions.question: Explain why the highest power determines the shape of the graph.
questions.answer: Because it controls the curvature of the equation.

questions.question: Why can a quadratic equation have repeated roots?
questions.answer: Because the discriminant can be zero.

questions.question: Explain why roots correspond to x-intercepts.
questions.answer: Because roots occur where y = 0.

questions.question: Why is the axis of symmetry important in graph analysis?
questions.answer: Because it divides the parabola into two equal halves.

questions.question: Why does a quadratic graph have only one turning point?
questions.answer: Because the degree is two.

questions.question: Explain the importance of quadratic equations in mathematics.
questions.answer: Because they model many real-life and theoretical problems.
